meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual
Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Legislative and senior managers
family.definition: This major group comprises senior management occupations, including legislators and senior managers in the public and private sectors.
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

units.title: Legislators
units.definition: Legislators participate in the activities of a federal, provincial, territorial or local government legislative body or executive council, band council or school board as elected or appointed members.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Legislators participate in the activities of a federal, provincial, territorial or local government legislative body or executive council, band council or school board as elected or appointed members.
main_duties: Enact, amend or repeal laws and regulations
Participate in developing or amending government policies, programs or procedures
Represent their government at local, national and international meetings and conferences
Respond to matters of concern to constituents or the general public

May serve as minister responsible for a government department or agency and direct senior government managers and officials in the implementation of government policy and the management of that department or agency.
employment_requirements: Election to a legislative body, or appointment to positions such as senator, lieutenant-governor or governor general, is required.

example_titles.job_title_text: Member of the House of Assembly (MHA)

exclusions.job_title: chief executive officer (CEO) - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations

core_competencies.statement: to clearly present arguments, advocate for policies and effectively communicate with constituents and the media
to analyze complex issues, evaluate policy impacts and develop solutions that benefit constituents
to understand proposed legislation, existing laws and regulatory frameworks when making informed decisions on enacting, amending or repealing laws and regulations
to balance multiple legislative priorities, track ongoing legislation and engage in debates and negotiations
to represent their government at local, national and international meetings and conferences

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Public Affairs and Government Relations
core_competencies.statement: to understand governance structures, intergovernmental dynamics and stakeholder interests in order to shape policies that reflect constituents’ needs

units.title: Senior government managers and officials
units.definition: Senior government managers and officials plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, the major activities of municipal or regional governments or of provincial, territorial or federal departments, boards, agencies or commissions. They establish the direction to be taken by these organizations in accordance with legislation and policies made by elected representatives or legislative bodies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Senior government managers and officials plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, the major activities of municipal or regional governments or of provincial, territorial or federal departments, boards, agencies or commissions. They establish the direction to be taken by these organizations in accordance with legislation and policies made by elected representatives or legislative bodies.
main_duties: Establish objectives for the organization in accordance with government legislation and policy, formulate or approve and evaluate programs and procedures alone or in conjunction with senior government committees
Advise elected representatives on policy questions and refer major policy matters to these representatives for final decision
Recommend, review, evaluate and approve documents, briefs and reports submitted by middle managers and senior staff members
Co-ordinate department activities with other senior government managers and officials
Make presentations to legislative and other government committees regarding policies, programs or budgets
Allocate human and financial resources to implement organizational policies and programs
Establish financial and administrative controls.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma is usually required.
A graduate degree in a related field may be required.
Several years of managerial experience in the public or private sector are required.
additional_information: There is mobility among senior management occupations.

example_titles.job_title_text: clerk of the House of Commons
secretary to the council - government services
sergeant-at-arms of the House of Commons

core_competencies.statement: to assess complex issues, anticipate potential challenges and evaluate different strategies in order to set organizational direction, respond to emerging issues and implement policies efficiently
to evaluate how different departments or agencies interact, identify inefficiencies and design systems that enhance overall performance and effectiveness
to understand written proposals, reports and program details in order to formulate or approve programs and procedures
to identify and group various elements in a logical sequence in order to develop, assess and refine programs and procedures effectively
to guide, influence and inspire teams toward achieving public policy objectives and ensuring the efficient delivery of government services
to plan and allocate resources efficiently, implement strategic initiatives and ensure organizational goals are met in accordance with legislative and policy frameworks

units.title: Senior managers - financial, communications and other business services
units.definition: Senior managers in financial, communications and other business services are usually appointed by a board of directors, to which they report. They work either alone or in conjunction with the board of directors to develop and establish objectives for the company, and to develop or approve policies and programs. They plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, the operations of their organization in relation to established objectives. They work in establishments throughout the telecommunications, finance, insurance, real estate, and data processing, hosting and related services industries as well as other business service industries or they may own and operate their own business.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Senior managers - financial, communications and other business services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Senior managers - financial, communications and other business services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Senior managers in financial, communications and other business services are usually appointed by a board of directors, to which they report. They work either alone or in conjunction with the board of directors to develop and establish objectives for the company, and to develop or approve policies and programs. They plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, the operations of their organization in relation to established objectives.
main_duties: Determine the company's mission and strategic direction as conveyed through policies and concrete objectives which are met through the effective management of human, financial and material resources
Authorize and organize the establishment of major departments and associated senior staff positions

Allocate material, human and financial resources to implement organizational policies and programs; establish financial and administrative controls; formulate and approve promotional campaigns; and approve overall human resources planning
Select middle managers, directors or other executive staff; delegate the necessary authority to them and create optimum working conditions
Represent the organization, or delegate representatives to act on behalf of the organization, in negotiations or other official functions.
employment_requirements: A university degree in business administration, commerce, computer science or other discipline related to the service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in financial, communications or other business services are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or service is possible through specific university training in that area or through previous experience.
Senior managers in finance usually require a professional accounting or financial designation.
additional_information: There is mobility among senior management occupations.
Senior managers in this unit group may specialize in areas such as finance, marketing or human resources or in a particular service area.
workplaces_employers: Establishments throughout the telecommunications, finance, insurance, real estate, data processing, hosting and related services industries

core_competencies.statement: to evaluate situations, anticipate challenges and develop effective strategies to address issues and achieve objectives
to assess the effectiveness of policies, programs or operational procedures and understand how different components of the organization interact in order to identify areas for improvement
to review written business proposals, investment analyses and feasibility studies when considering new ventures or investment opportunities
to recognize emerging risks, operational inefficiencies or strategic challenges early, ensuring timely decision-making that protects organizational and stakeholder interests
to establish clear goals, define the company's mission and develop a strategic direction for the organization
to oversee operations, develop strategies and ensure the organization meets its objectives

units.title: Senior managers - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations
units.definition: Senior managers in health, education, social and community services and membership organizations plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, membership and other organizations or institutions that deliver health, education, social or community services. They formulate policies which establish the direction to be taken by these organizations, either alone or in conjunction with a board of directors. They are employed in health care organizations, educational services, social and community services and membership organizations or they may own and operate their own business.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Senior managers - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Senior managers - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Senior managers in health, education, social and community services and membership organizations plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, membership and other organizations or institutions that deliver health, education, social or community services. They formulate policies which establish the direction to be taken by these organizations, either alone or in conjunction with a board of directors.
main_duties: Establish objectives for the organization or institution and formulate or approve policies and programs
Authorize and organize the establishment of major departments and associated senior staff positions

Allocate material, human and financial resources to implement organizational policies and programs; establish financial and administrative controls; formulate and approve promotional campaigns; and approve overall personnel planning
Select middle managers, directors or other executive staff
Coordinate the work of regions, divisions or departments
Represent the organization, or delegate representatives to act on behalf of the organization, in negotiations or other official functions.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business administration, hospital administration, finance or other discipline related to the service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in a related institution or organization are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or service is possible through specific university or college training in that area or through previous experience.
Senior managers in finance usually require a professional accounting designation.
additional_information: There is mobility among senior management occupations.
Senior managers in this unit group may specialize in areas such as finance, marketing, human resources or in a particular service area.
workplaces_employers: Organizations that deliver health, education, social or community services

core_competencies.statement: to analyze complex issues, develop policies and make informed decisions by assessing factors, anticipating challenges and devising solutions to enhance service delivery
to identify inefficiencies, streamline operations and ensure different parts of the organization work together effectively
to interact with a wide range of stakeholders including staff, clients, government agencies, donors and community members
to categorize and prioritize offerings based on factors such as community needs, funding availability and organizational mission to ensure effective resource allocation and service delivery

to strategically plan and make decisions in order to advance the organization's mission and objectives and to advocate for the needs and interests of the organization, clients and the broader community
to ensure the organization operates efficiently, adheres to budgets and meets objectives in delivering health, education or community services

units.title: Senior managers - trade, broadcasting and other services
units.definition: Senior managers in trade, broadcasting and other customer services plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, trade, broadcasting and other service industry companies. They formulate policies which establish the direction to be taken by these companies, either alone or in conjunction with a board of directors. They work in establishments in broadcasting and related media services, wholesale trade, retail trade, accommodation and food service, and other services or they may own and operate their own business.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Senior managers - trade, broadcasting and other services
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Senior managers - trade, broadcasting and other services
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Senior managers in trade, broadcasting and other customer services plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, trade, broadcasting and other service industry companies. They formulate policies which establish the direction to be taken by these companies, either alone or in conjunction with a board of directors.
main_duties: Establish objectives for the company and formulate or approve policies and programs
Authorize and organize the establishment of major departments and associated senior staff positions

Allocate material, human and financial resources to implement organizational policies and programs; establish financial and administrative controls; formulate and approve promotional campaigns; and approve overall personnel planning
Select middle managers, directors or other executive staff
Coordinate the work of regions, divisions or departments
Represent the company, or delegate representatives to act on behalf of the company, in negotiations or other official functions.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business administration, finance or other discipline related to the service provided is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in trade, broadcasting or other service are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or service is possible through specific university or college training in that area or through previous experience.
Senior managers in finance usually require a professional accounting designation.
additional_information: There is mobility among senior management occupations.
Senior managers in this unit group may specialize in areas such as finance, marketing or human resources or in the sale of a particular product or provision of a particular service.
workplaces_employers: Establishments in broadcasting and related media services

core_competencies.statement: to analyze market trends, evaluate business challenges and develop effective strategies while adapting to changing conditions in trade, broadcasting and service industries
to optimize the interactions between different components of the organization and assess the performance of policies, programs and operational processes
to articulate the company's position, negotiate terms and reach agreements that align with organizational goals and objectives
to categorize and prioritize organizational objectives based on market trends, customer needs and competitive pressures
to motivate and engage teams, achieve organizational objectives and embrace emerging technologies and content formats while staying ahead of competitors and capturing audience attention
to drive operational efficiency, handle financial planning and ensure the organization meets its objectives and adapts to industry changes

units.title: Senior managers - construction, transportation, production and utilities
units.definition: Senior managers in construction, transportation, production and utilities plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, the overall operations of goods production, utility, transportation and construction companies. They formulate policies which establish the direction to be taken by these companies, either alone or in conjunction with other members of a board of directors. They work in establishments throughout the following industries: fishing, forestry, logging and agriculture; mining, oil and gas extraction; construction; transportation and warehousing; printing; manufacturing; and utilities or they may own and operate their own business.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Management occupations (0).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Senior managers - construction, transportation, production and utilities
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Senior managers in construction, transportation, production and utilities plan, organize, direct, control and evaluate, through middle managers, the overall operations of goods production, utility, transportation and construction companies. They formulate policies which establish the direction to be taken by these companies, either alone or in conjunction with other members of a board of directors.
main_duties: Establish objectives for the company and formulate or approve policies and programs
Authorize and organize the establishment of major departments and associated senior staff positions

Allocate material, human and financial resources to implement company policies and programs; establish financial and administrative controls; formulate and approve promotional campaigns; and approve overall personnel planning
Select middle managers, directors or other executive staff
Coordinate the work of regions, divisions or departments
Represent the company, or delegate representatives to act on behalf of the company, in negotiations or other official functions.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in engineering, business administration, commerce or other discipline related to the company's product is usually required.
Several years of experience as a middle manager in goods production, utilities, transportation or construction are usually required.
Specialization in a particular functional area or product is possible through specific university or college training in that area or through previous experience.
Senior managers in finance usually require a professional accounting designation.
additional_information: There is mobility among senior management occupations.
Senior managers in this unit group may specialize in areas such as finance, marketing or human resources or in a particular product area.

core_competencies.statement: to navigate challenges, analyze complex operational issues, optimize processes and make strategic decisions that impact production, transportation, construction and utility operations
to optimize interactions between various components of operations by assessing the effectiveness of policies, programs and operational procedures
to communicate company policies, objectives and strategies to middle managers, employees and other stakeholders
to oversee projects by organizing complex data, prioritizing tasks and adapting to changing project needs
to set the vision and direction of the organization and to engage teams in order to achieve organizational objectives
to drive operational efficiency, handle financial planning and ensure the company meets its objectives while adapting to industry changes
